title: String
subtitle: Text inside quotes
goal: Learn how JavaScript stores text values.
explanation: String is the data type for text.
All words, sentences, messages, and names in JavaScript are stored as Strings.
To tell JavaScript that something is text, you must wrap it in quotes.
Strings can use double quotes, single quotes, or backticks.
Without quotes, JavaScript treats the word as a variable name.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: A String is like writing on a sign. As long as the text is inside quotes, JavaScript knows it is just text.

remember: Text must be wrapped in quotes.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: If you write Vitalii without quotes, JavaScript will look for a variable with that name instead of treating it as text.

quiz.question: Which value has the String type?
